title: Sky Demon City
content:
Overview
[
]

Sky Demon City is the capital of the Sky Demon Country, located in the northern part of the Demon Spirit Land. It is surrounded by countless intersecting mountains that, when viewed from above, resemble nine dragons coiling protectively around the city. Each dragon head points toward Dragon Lake, a secluded and heavily guarded body of water that houses the Ancient Demon Temple, the holy symbol of the Sky Demon Country. The city itself lies directly in front of this sacred lake, reinforcing its spiritual and strategic importance.

More precisely, Sky Demon City is composed of nine cities of similar size to Ancient Demon City, arranged in the shape of an eight divinatory trigram with the royal palace at the center. The palace is encircled by a river and linked to the surrounding cities by eight bridges. Within the palace, the Demon Emperor resides among exquisite pavilions, while each of the eight outer cities displays its own distinct architectural style.

The city is often shrouded in a faint green mist known as 
dragon energy
, emanating from the surrounding mountain ranges. Cultivators believe this energy enhances cultivation, and from a distance, the city appears like a colossal beast awakening as the mist disperses with the dawn of daily life.

Sky Demon City is also a hub of transportation and military logistics. Outside its walls stand 18 transfer arrays, guarded year-round by demon soldiers. Eight are designated for civilians such as merchants, while ten are reserved for individuals of high status. The 14th transfer array is exclusively for demon generals, whose activation summons these powerful figures—commanders of vast armies and wielders of formidable spells.

Politically and militarily, the city is unmatched. It houses eight commander-in-chiefs and eight vice commander-in-chiefs, deployed during wartime under the direct leadership of the Demon Emperor himself. The imperial square near the Sky Demon Gate serves as the venue for major events such as the demon general competition. This ten-thousand-foot square is protected by powerful formations to prevent destruction, with seating arranged hierarchically: the Demon Emperor at the top, multi-tiered sections for commanders, and expansive areas for officials and spectators. The atmosphere during these competitions is tense, filled with speculation and anticipation as cultivators and generals vie for supremacy.

A defining feature of the imperial city is the Sky Demon Drum, a colossal war drum used only for sacred purposes—worshiping ancestors, opening Dragon Lake, or marking significant events. Its thunderous strikes reverberate across the city, awakening its inhabitants and signaling moments of great importance. The drum has played pivotal roles in extraordinary events, such as when Wang Lin activated it to unleash immense power and defy the heavens, engulfing the city in celestial and demonic energies.

Sky Demon City has endured catastrophic trials. The Emperor’s sword once acted autonomously, soaring through the skies to attack parts of the city, forcing demon generals to intervene. Divine punishments have also struck, including the descent of the heavens’ might, which crushed the city into a deep basin. Though devastated, the city was rebuilt over time, restoring its grandeur and reinforcing its resilience. In recent history, it has been the center of large-scale conflicts, particularly the war between the Sky Demon Country and the Fire Demon Country. Despite heavy losses, the city remains a symbol of strength, with the Demon Emperor and his generals actively leading its defense.

Today, Sky Demon City stands as a vast, fortified metropolis—an epicenter of political authority, military might, and spiritual reverence. It is alive with the presence of demon generals, commanders, cultivators, and officials, its history marked by divine trials, legendary artifacts, and the unyielding will of its people. More than a capital, it is the beating heart of the Sky Demon Country, embodying its glory, resilience, and power
